summary: The original valid-checksum event-tail crash is fixed, and malformed unknown event kinds now reject without mutating. SIM-005 is still not accepted because a valid-checksum saved DomainEvent with an unsupported schemaVersion is silently loaded and coerced to schemaVersion 1 instead of being rejected as a versioned save-tail schema error.

tests_run.result: Writer fix handoff validates against project/schemas/handoff.schema.json.
6 test files passed; 31 tests passed; existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.
Node/worker hello hash 61e0ba1c matched; command/query hash d275faa4 matched; save/load hash 28074edd matched; save/load byte length 10920.
Recursive workspace typecheck passed, including apps/web declaration coverage.

tests_run.command: Prior QA repro: valid-checksum unknown event kind through loadSaveV1
tests_run.result: Resolved: returned status rejected with original runtime hash a38362fd and reason invalid-schema at body.eventTail[0].event.kind; no THREW output.

tests_run.command: New QA repro: valid-checksum known event kind with event schemaVersion 999 through loadSaveV1
tests_run.result: Still failing: returned status loaded with loadedEventSchema 1, silently accepting unsupported saved event schemaVersion 999.
Architecture check, sim determinism, 13 root Vitest files/52 tests, and web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed; existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning appeared.
All matched files use Prettier code style.

decisions: The first REQUEST_CHANGES blocker is fixed for unknown event kinds and malformed event fields: the loader now returns structured invalid-schema reasons and preserves the original runtime.
The worker-compatible adapter declaration has been refreshed for runSaveLoadCanaryInWorkerCompatibleAdapter and typecheck passes.

Because SIM-005 is R3 save/schema work and event tails are versioned DomainEvent DTOs, an unsupported event schemaVersion must be explicitly rejected or migrated; silently coercing it to v1 is not acceptable.
risks: The current parser does not check saved DomainEvent schemaVersion. Future or corrupted event-tail DTOs with known kind names can be accepted as v1, which weakens save compatibility guarantees even though the authoritative world snapshot is not mutated.

blockers.repro: Create a valid-checksum save envelope with eventTail [{ sequence: 1, event: { schemaVersion: 999, kind: 'sim.state-hash-verified', commandId: 'bad.version', actor: { kind: 'system', id: 'save-contract' }, day: 0, revision: 0, stateHash: saved.stateHash } }] and load it with matching expected content/scenario. loadSaveV1 returns status loaded and runtime.eventTail[0].schemaVersion is 1.
blockers.impact: Unsupported saved DomainEvent schema versions are silently accepted and coerced, contrary to the explicit v1-only compatibility/rejection model for save tails.
blockers.necessary_fix: Validate event.schemaVersion === 1 for every saved DomainEvent before constructing the loaded runtime, return an invalid-schema SaveLoadRejectionReasonV1 on mismatch, preserve the original runtime, and add a regression test for a valid-checksum known event kind with unsupported event schemaVersion.

requested_action: Please reject or explicitly migrate unsupported saved DomainEvent schemaVersion values in the sim-core event-tail load parser, add a regression test for schemaVersion 999 on a known event kind, then rerun the SIM-005 validation matrix.
